title: Performance Measurement: A portfolio's alpha is a measure of its risk-adjusted performance,...
summary: Calculate the alpha of a security under CAPM by comparing its actual return to the CAPM-predicted expected return.
prompt: A portfolio's alpha is a measure of its risk-adjusted performance, indicating how much better or worse the portfolio performed compared to its expected return based on its beta.
solution: Alpha quantifies performance on a risk-adjusted basis by comparing the actual return of a portfolio to the return predicted by the CAPM or other asset pricing models, based on its beta. A positive alpha indicates the portfolio has outperformed its expected return, while a negative alpha suggests underperformance. This measure is crucial for evaluating the effectiveness of portfolio management and investment strategies.
